“Physco “ 1960 This film directed by Alfred Hitchcock was one of the best of his collection. I got to watch this film in the 1960s when it was still relatively new and if you’re an offer Hitchcock fan I highly recommend this film to you. This film stars Janet Lee Vera miles and Anthony Perkins who portrays Norman Bates. Without giving too much away I’m going to leave it at that. This film was suitable for all ages.

This is a great but flawed movie. The driving looks really fake. The voice over inner monologue is not necessary and ruins the mood, we get what they’re thinking without spelling it out to us. There are plot holes like changing cars in front of the cop who would see the new car to follow her. Also she acts so guilty, surely she’d be able to hide her guilt better. The music is good but it’s so overused it becomes repetitive in the first act. Some of the pacing is slow like the endless cleaning up. The finale in the basement is pretty goofy and all the exposition at the end is superfluous. But there is a lot to like, phenomenal direction, excellent writing, very good acting, engaging characters etc. The conversations are so engaging and that is where this film is really excellent. For every moment of brilliance there are elements I don't like. It’s an excellent story but when you know the ending it loses the impact and repeat watching is less engaging.
Such a classic movie. Definitely worth watching.

Not my favorite Hitchcock film. The first act with Janet Leigh is engaging. But after she is murdered and disposed of, the movie kind of drags. It really doesn't get better until the couple arrives at the motel in the end. Still, it's a good movie overall.
truly revolutionary. just incredible. greatest horror film ever.

Yeah yeah, everyone knows this movie. Critically acclaimed this, critically acclaimed that. Many say it's one of the scariest movies of all time. Yeah, well, this is the 21st century, and things like this just aren't anymore. Was I scared? Hell no. BTW, I don't like black and white movies. Was I a little too harsh in this rating? Um, I don't know. It's whatever.

Existem pouquíssimos filmes que eu posso, sem hesitar, chamar de perfeitos. Esse é um deles. A direção, a fotografia, a trilha sonora, as atuações, o roteiro... tudo nesse filme foi perfeitamente planejado e arquitetado para os oferecer umas das mais icônicas e memoráveis experiências cinematográficas de terror. Dirigido pelo Mestre do Suspense, "Psicose" moldou o terror moderno e serviu de inspiração e referência para o gênero por décadas. Em todos os aspectos, um título incontornável para fãs de terror, cinéfilos em busca de conhecer os clássicos ou, simplesmente, apreciadores de entretenimento da mais altíssima qualidade.

A stellar horror even today. The music is fantastic. Anthony Perkins plays a great psycho and the investigator Arbogast (Martin Balsam) had a lot to do adding the suspense, suspense which is the best thing this movie accomplishes.

Hitchcock scariest film is a blast on the big screen. The picture is crisp, the sound design is superb, Hermann’s score is screechy and relentless, and the actors are in perfect sync with Hitch’s rhythms. He’s in complete control of the dark material.

This was probably one of the very first Alfred Hitchcock creations that I had watched, and it opened up an entire new world of creepy films. Though, I do not agree with Hitchcock’s behaviours toward actresses during the filming of his movies by far, he did create some chilling films. This, being one of them. I remember the first time I had ever watched this movie, as a teenager on a late August night, with a big bag of popcorn. I was not disappointed. The movie lived up to its name, and surpassed it. I was spooked and freaked out. As an introduction to both the world of classic films and classic horror, you can not go wrong with starting with Alfred Hitchcock’s Psycho.

Incredible performances by Perkins and Leigh, great story, well-paced, beautiful cinematography. The film still holds up so well after all these years. Suspenseful and intriguing. One of the greatest movies of all time and Anthony Perkins performance as Norman Bates is absolutely brilliant.

Hitch created one of those, "Before & After" works of art; there were horror movies before Psycho.... it stands as a lone achievement, it's that good, one that will be talked about forever, particularly among filmmakers, and especially those who want to make horror. An absolute masterpiece. And to think, he shot it with his TV crew!

We feel like it set the tone for these types of films going forward. Sound effects and music were great aspects of this movie!
This will always be a Classic Slasher Horror Thriller film. 🔥🔥

Alfred Hitchcock’s Psycho (1960) is a deliciously unsettling thriller that’s as fresh today as it was at its premiere. Anthony Perkins’ Norman Bates is a complex, creepy delight…his nervous smile hides a rotten core. The shower scene? A sharp, pulpy shock that still leaves audiences splattered with awe. Bernard Herrmann’s screeching score is the perfect seasoning, amplifying every tense moment. Hitchcock masterfully squeezes every drop of suspense from this tale of secrets and madness. Psycho is a perfectly ripe classic…bite into it, but brace for the aftertaste.
My favorite thriller! This movie is very artistic and it aged well.

There really was nothing I could point out that was bad about this film. This was revolutionary to the horror drama. It really was not an out of date film either. Like, when I compared the new Superman film to the old one, you can see how dated it was. But for me, watching it for the first time, I was still tense and creepy. Well done by Alfred Hitchcock!

Still one of the best horrors of all time in my opinion and favorite movies of all time!
You may get bored but follow hichcocks advice watch the whole thing. One of the best horror movies a absolute must see if you like movies.

Купить несчастье за 40`000$ Мега-база от основателя киносаспенса? Почти. Все дело в том, что «Психо» выворачивается вообще в другой жанр во второй половине фильма, причем настолько резко и неожиданно в другую сторону, что кажется, будто начал смотреть другое кино. Узрев завязку, кажется, что тебя ждёт психологический триллер про паранойю и ложную тревогу, но спустя некоторое время кино становится историей ужастиком про убийцу-маньяка с раздвоением личности. Сам фильм неплохой, просто первый вариант более нетривиален и любопытен, нежели еще одно «кино по Стивену Кингу» (тогда-то понятно что это было еще не так заезженно, но если сейчас смотреть, то сценарий сверх банален). Актёрам веришь, да может сейчас их игра и выглядит немного, так скажем, театрально, зато как же здесь Энтони Перкинс люто божит своей мимикой и интонациями. Аккомпанемент музыкального сопровождения отлично справляется с созданием атмосферы тревожности и давящего нагнетания. Финальный замес, конечно, могли сделать более продолжительным и не таким нелепым. Ну а так, даже сегодня можно посмотреть и кайфануть, хотя бы за счет актёрской игры. 8 из 10

Psycho - 10/10. Alfred Hitchcock’s Psycho is a trailblazing movie for the horror genre. For the time that it came out, Psycho pushed the boundaries as to what a scary movie could do and achieve. Based around a real life serial killer, the movie revolves around a peeping tom motel owner named Norman Bates. Along with his killing tendencies, he also has a really bad case of mommy issues. Marion Crane, an outlaw with a ton of cash on her, makes a bad decision by deciding to make a pitstop at the Bates Motel. This leads to her ultimate demise, which spirals into an investigation lead by Crane’s sister in hopes of tracking down her and finding out what happened. Hitchcock is a master of suspense. He was a person that understood how to create tension and scares, without having to show extreme amounts of violence or gore. Psycho is the perfect example of a movie that makes you imagine the terror i stead of seeing it. Much of the scary moments in the movie are left to our imagination in terms of the things we can’t see. Even with the now infamous shower scene, the camera never shows the knife plunging into Crane. Its the editing and visuals of her eyes and face in terror, along with the blood swirling down the drain that make you feel the magnitude of the situation. Its an amazing movie that still holds up after all these decades. Its one that even if you ate watching it for the first time, it surprises you and grips your attention from start to finish!
Masterpiece. Revolutionize thriller horror movies.
